summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an explosive New Year’s Eve show featuring a smoking Lazy Lightning / Supplication, stellar One More Saturday Night and fiery Scarlet / Fire.
review: The recording is praised as a 10/10, crisp soundboard transfer that lets the band’s high energy shine through. Fans highlight blistering jams like Lazy Lightning > Supplication, an inspired One More Saturday Night and a fiery Scarlet > Fire, while noting weaker moments on Truckin' and Casey Jones. Overall the performance is viewed as one of the stronger NYE shows of ’77 despite a few uneven spots.

ai_rating.rationale: Two enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise both the music and the pristine soundboard, while a single 3‑star review notes only modest issues; weighted average yields ~4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the transfer as crystal‑clear, 10/10 sound quality and the best available clarity for this show; no major flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, punchy first set leads into a second set with high‑octane jams; the middle of the show shines while the later portions lose some momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar is described as explosive and in the zone, especially on Lazy Lightning/Supplication and Wharf Rat > Not Fade Away; attempts a longer solo on Casey Jones that feels premature.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a great performance on Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are praised as pounding the show, though Bobby (Mickey) gets hoarse toward the end.
